title: A resolution condemning the commutation of the death sentence of Anthony George Battle granted by President Biden on December 23, 2024.
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary. (text: CR S140)
plain_summary: This resolution criticizes President Biden for changing Anthony George Battle's punishment from death to life in prison. Battle was sentenced to death for killing his wife, who was a Marine, and a prison guard by hitting them with a hammer. The Senate thinks Battle should have gotten the death penalty and believes President Biden made this decision for political reasons instead of doing what was right. The resolution officially states that the Senate does not agree with President Biden's choice to save Battle from execution.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary. (text: CR S140)
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1 Sense of the Senate Regarding the Commutation of the Death Sentence of Anthony George Battle Granted by President Biden on December 23, 2024
sections.text: The Senate believes that President Joseph R. Biden hurt the rule of law and denied justice to victims when he changed Anthony George Battle's death sentence to life in prison on December twenty third, twenty twenty four. Anthony Battle was found guilty of murdering his wife, who was a U.S. Marine, and was sentenced to life in prison. While Battle was serving his life sentence at Atlanta Federal Prison, he murdered a thirty one year old prison guard named D'Antonio Washington by hitting Washington in the back of the head over and over with a ball peen hammer (a type of hammer with a rounded end). When investigators questioned Battle, he showed no regret and said that he was happy he killed Washington. This decision to change Battle's sentence is a terrible insult to the victims of Anthony Battle's crimes. President Biden claimed that he changed the death sentences of Anthony Battle and thirty six other murderers because he is against the death penalty on principle, but he refused to change the death sentences of the three most controversial death row prisoners. This shows that President Biden was motivated by politics, not principles. The Senate completely condemns this decision to change Battle's sentence.
